affordances.name: Calibrated struggle for transfer
affordances.mechanism: When fluency would be mistaken for mastery, add retrieval, spacing, problem disaggregation, or explanation work that creates productive struggle without exceeding working memory or becoming friction for its own sake.

use_when: Passive review, easy fluency, or immediate performance is masking whether understanding will transfer.
The goal is durable retention or flexible use under new conditions.
Support can be calibrated so the challenge stretches capability without overwhelming it.
do_not_use_when: Difficulty is being added as proof of seriousness rather than learning value.
The challenge exceeds cognitive capacity and prevents new structures from forming.
The team is already delaying action through unnecessary analysis or data gathering.
case_evidence_needed: The current fluency or ease signal that may be misleading.
The transfer or retention outcome the difficulty should improve.
The planned difficulty and why it is productive.
The overload or delay boundary that would make the difficulty undesirable.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the learning weakness, the desirable difficulty, the transfer signal, and the stop condition where struggle becomes overload or waste.
good_output_shape: Names the transfer or retention test.
Defines when the difficulty should be reduced or stopped.

diagnostic_questions: What looks easy now but may fail under retrieval or transfer?
What difficulty would strengthen the skill rather than just consume time?
Where does productive struggle become overload or delay?
misuse_guards: Do not confuse harder with better.
Do not use desirable difficulty to justify boiling the ocean.
Do not remove support entirely when calibrated support is what makes the difficulty productive.

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when fluency would otherwise be mistaken for mastery
Works when the challenge is productive precisely because it strengthens transfer

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 12 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports desirable difficulty as calibrated effort for retention and transfer, with clear overload and friction guards.
dropped_material: Did not extract deep work, Feynman, or problem disaggregation as separate affordances here.
Kept negotiation and AI examples as context only.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card adds useful productive-struggle checks without rewarding pain.

absence_records.reason: The source explicitly warns against confusing harder with better; difficulty must create learning value.
The source says excessive difficulty produces overload, analysis paralysis, or wasted work, so PR49 must not promote all friction as rigor.
The source says the useful difficulty should sit in crafting clear communication, not in forcing the audience to decipher avoidable confusion.

source_evidence.source_quote: The necessary difficulty must be borne by the communicator in crafting the clear message, not by the audience in deciphering it.
introducing strategic, effortful challenges and struggle

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 12 source reading found strong reviewed depth for calibrated productive difficulty.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should check whether this card helps packets separate productive stretch from needless friction.
